name: BLADDER EXSTROPHY
definition: EPISPADIA-EXSTROPHY COMPLEX failure of lower abdominal wall + anterior bladder wall to close normally persistent large cloacal membrane without rupture

demographics.Etiology: incomplete retraction of cloacal membrane prevents normal midline migration of

dominant_finding: persistently "absent" urinary bladder (HALLMARK) → urine is released immediately into amniotic cavity
distribution: irregular thickening of everted posterior bladder wall ← inflammation of exposed mucosa
discriminating_features: persistently "absent" urinary bladder (HALLMARK) → urine is released immediately into amniotic cavity
irregular thickening of everted posterior bladder wall ← inflammation of exposed mucosa
ventral defect of infraumbilical abdominal wall
pubic diastasis = widening of pubic symphysis

general: persistently "absent" urinary bladder (HALLMARK) → urine is released immediately into amniotic cavity
irregular thickening of everted posterior bladder wall ← inflammation of exposed mucosa
ventral defect of infraumbilical abdominal wall
pubic diastasis = widening of pubic symphysis

clinical.Cx: urinary incontinence, infertility, pyelonephritis, bladder carcinoma (4%)
clinical.Rx: primary closure, bladder excision with urinary diversion
